name: cpp
description: Modern C++ conventions (C++20/23), Core Guidelines, RAII, smart pointers, testing, and tooling for production code

decisions.decision: Target C++20 as baseline; adopt C++23 features via feature-test macros; do not use C++20 modules yet
decisions.rationale: C++20 has mature compiler support across GCC, Clang, and MSVC. C++23 features like std::expected and std::print are valuable but not universally available. C++20 modules lack ecosystem maturity (major libraries have not adopted them, cross-compiler interop is incomplete).
decisions.details:
Set CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD 20, CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD_REQUIRED ON, CMAKE_CXX_EXTENSIONS OFF. Guard C++23 features via __cpp_lib_* macros.
Adopt: concepts, ranges, std::format, std::jthread, <=>, designated initializers, constexpr.
No C++20 modules yet; use traditional headers.

decisions.decision: Use std::expected for recoverable errors; exceptions only for truly unrecoverable conditions
decisions.rationale: std::expected (C++23) provides type-safe error handling without hidden control flow or exception overhead. It encodes success and failure in the type system and supports monadic chaining (and_then, transform, or_else). Exceptions remain appropriate for programming errors and conditions that cannot be handled locally.
decisions.details:
std::expected<T, E> at API boundaries. Project-wide error enum for E.
Never .value() without has_value(). Chain: .and_then().transform().or_else().
Exceptions for: constructor failures, unrecoverable errors, multi-level unwinding.
Polyfill: tl::expected or std::optional for simple cases.

decisions.decision: Ban raw new/delete; use smart pointers, containers, span, and string_view; enforce via Core Guidelines clang-tidy checks
decisions.rationale: Memory safety is under regulatory scrutiny (CISA/NSA guidance). Modern C++ can be made memory-safe through disciplined use of RAII, containers, and views. The C++ Core Guidelines codify these practices with tooling enforcement via clang-tidy.
decisions.details:
Prefer: stack > unique_ptr > shared_ptr > weak_ptr > raw ptr/ref (non-owning). Use make_unique/make_shared. Non-owning params: T& or const T&.
std::span for buffers, std::string_view for read-only strings, std::variant over unions, range-for over index loops.
Enforce via cppcoreguidelines-* in clang-tidy.

decisions.decision: Follow the C++ Core Guidelines as the primary reference; enforce via clang-tidy cppcoreguidelines-* checks
decisions.rationale: The Core Guidelines (Stroustrup/Sutter) are a living document providing practical rules for safe, efficient C++. They are enforced by clang-tidy and supported by the Guidelines Support Library (GSL). Following them systematically addresses the most common C++ pitfalls.
decisions.details:
Enable cppcoreguidelines-* in clang-tidy. Consider Microsoft GSL: gsl::not_null<T>, gsl::narrow<T>.
Principles: safe subset of superset, static type safety, RAII resource safety, zero-overhead abstractions.

decisions.decision: Use clang-tidy with broad checks enabled minus vendor-specific; cppcheck as complement
decisions.rationale: clang-tidy integrates with the compilation database for accurate analysis. Starting with all checks enabled and selectively disabling vendor-specific ones gives maximum coverage. cppcheck catches different classes of bugs with a low false-positive rate.
decisions.details:
Checks: * then disable: -abseil-*, -altera-*, -android-*, -fuchsia-*, -google-*, -llvm*, -zircon-*.
Key: bugprone-*, cert-*, cppcoreguidelines-*, clang-analyzer-*, modernize-*, performance-*, readability-*.
Integrate via CMAKE_CXX_CLANG_TIDY. Run cppcheck as complement. Place .clang-tidy at root.

decisions.decision: Choose Catch2 or Google Test as the test framework; use CTest as the runner; add a mocking library as needed
decisions.rationale: Catch2 provides expressive BDD-style tests with low setup overhead. Google Test provides comprehensive features with built-in mocking via GoogleMock. Both are production-proven. CTest integrates naturally with CMake regardless of framework choice.
decisions.details:
Catch2: library, FetchContent/vcpkg, BDD-style, generators, matchers, benchmarks. Mock via Trompeloeil or FakeIt.
GTest: built-in GoogleMock, good IDE support. Choose for large teams with existing infra.
Property-based: RapidCheck. One test per source in tests/. Run via CTest.

decisions.decision: Three sanitizer CI jobs: ASan+UBSan, TSan, and optionally MSan; define CMake presets for each
decisions.rationale: Sanitizers catch memory safety and concurrency bugs that tests alone miss. ASan, TSan, and MSan are mutually exclusive — they must run as separate CI jobs. CMake presets make sanitizer configurations reusable.
decisions.details:
Job 1: -fsanitize=address,undefined -fno-omit-frame-pointer. Job 2: -fsanitize=thread. Job 3: -fsanitize=memory (Clang-only).
Define CMakePresets.json per sanitizer. Rebuild all deps with same flags. Enable in Debug. Set ASAN_OPTIONS/UBSAN_OPTIONS for diagnostics.

decisions.decision: Use modern CMake with target-based configuration, CMakePresets.json, and Ninja as generator
decisions.rationale: CMake is the de facto standard for C++ builds. Target-based commands (target_*) replace error-prone directory-scoped settings. CMakePresets provide reproducible, shareable build configurations. Ninja is significantly faster than Make for incremental builds.
decisions.details:
Target-centric: target_link_libraries, target_include_directories + PRIVATE/PUBLIC/INTERFACE. Generator expressions for portability. Avoid set(CMAKE_CXX_FLAGS).
CMakePresets.json: hidden base presets, inherit for variants. Configure/build/test types.
Deps: find_package (system), FetchContent (small), vcpkg (large).

decisions.decision: Use a package manager (vcpkg or Conan) for dependency management; choose based on project needs
decisions.rationale: vcpkg provides tight CMake integration and simple manifest mode. Conan provides better cross-compilation profiles and private package hosting. Both are production-quality. For small projects, CMake FetchContent may be sufficient.
decisions.details:
vcpkg: vcpkg.json at root, wire via CMakePresets.json toolchain. Built from source (ABI-safe). Binary caching for CI.
Conan: large projects, embedded, internal registries. Lockfiles + profiles for cross-compilation.
Small/header-only: FetchContent suffices.

decisions.decision: Use clang-format with a project-specific .clang-format config; enforce in CI
decisions.rationale: clang-format is the universal standard for C++ formatting. Committed config eliminates formatting debates. CI enforcement prevents style drift.
decisions.details:
.clang-format at root. Base: LLVM, Google, or Chromium. Customize minimally.
CI: clang-format --dry-run --Werror. Pre-commit hooks recommended.
Opt out: // clang-format off / on.

decisions.decision: Adopt concepts, ranges, std::format, std::jthread, and three-way comparison in all new code
decisions.rationale: These C++20 features are production-ready with mature compiler support. Concepts replace SFINAE with clean constraint syntax. Ranges provide composable data pipelines. jthread auto-joins with cooperative cancellation. All reduce boilerplate and improve safety.
decisions.details: Concepts for all template constraints (replaces enable_if/SFINAE). Ranges + views for pipelines; C++23 ranges::to() materializes. std::format/std::print for type-safe formatting ({fmt} as polyfill). std::jthread over std::thread (RAII, auto-join). <=> for comparison boilerplate. Designated initializers: Point{.x=1, .y=2}.

decisions.decision: Public headers in include/project_name/, private sources in src/, tests in tests/; CMakePresets.json for build configuration
decisions.rationale: Namespaced include paths prevent header collisions during installation. Separating public and private headers enforces API boundaries. CMakePresets.json provides reproducible builds across developer machines and CI.
decisions.details: Layout: CMakeLists.txt, CMakePresets.json (VCS), CMakeUserPresets.json (gitignore), vcpkg.json, .clang-format, .clang-tidy, include/project_name/ (public headers), src/ (impl + private headers), tests/, cmake/ (custom modules), docs/.
